sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Lecce has shown decent form in their last 5 matches with no losses, managing 2 wins and 3 draws, though their attacking output has been limited. Milan, on the other hand, has had a mixed bag with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, but they’ve demonstrated strong attacking potential in certain games like their 9-0 win over Perth Glory. This suggests Milan might have an edge in offensive capabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Milan has dominated Lecce, winning both matches (3-0 and 3-2). Milan has scored 6 goals against Lecce in these games, showing their ability to exploit Lecce’s defense. Lecce has struggled to keep clean sheets against Milan, conceding in all recent matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Lecce’s season stats indicate a defensive solidity with no goals conceded yet and a high ranking in clean sheets, though their attack lacks potency (0 goals scored). Milan’s expected goals and big chances created in recent games suggest they can create scoring opportunities against Lecce’s defense. Milan’s higher possession and passing accuracy could control the game’s tempo.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Milan’s historical dominance over Lecce, combined with their ability to score late in games (as seen in head-to-heads), makes them favorites. Lecce’s home advantage and current unbeaten streak might keep the game close, but their lack of goals could hinder a positive result. The referee’s average of 4.5 yellow cards per game and both teams’ stats suggest a moderately physical match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Lecce and Milan at Via del Mare ended in a goalless draw. Milan dominated possession and created more dangerous opportunities, showing a clear intent to control the game. Lecce, on the other hand, held their ground defensively, managing to limit Milan’s finishing despite being under pressure for significant periods. Neither side managed to convert their chances, with both teams lacking the clinical edge in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Lecce
halftimeSections.text: Lecce showed resilience in the first half, focusing on a compact defensive structure to counter Milan’s attacking intent. They struggled to maintain consistent possession but were effective in winning duels, especially in the air, which helped disrupt Milan’s rhythm at times. Their entries into the final third were limited, and they failed to register a shot on target, indicating a lack of threat going forward. Players like Lassana Coulibaly and Tiago Gabriel stood out with their work rate and defensive contributions, providing a solid base for the team.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Milan
halftimeSections.text: Milan controlled much of the game in the first half, enjoying a significant share of possession and creating multiple entries into Lecce’s penalty area. They fashioned a couple of big chances but were let down by poor finishing, missing key opportunities to take the lead. Their expected goals were notably higher than Lecce’s, reflecting their attacking dominance. Players like Alexis Saelemaekers and Youssouf Fofana were instrumental in driving the team forward, while Santiago Giménez showed promise but couldn’t convert his chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Milan started the game with strong attacking intent, pushing forward with consistent pressure in the early minutes. Lecce had brief spells of momentum in the middle of the half, attempting to build play from the back and challenge Milan’s defense. However, Milan regained control with sustained attacking waves towards the latter stages of the half, creating a series of dangerous moments. Lecce showed some late resilience with a push in the closing minutes, but Milan ended the half on a high note with one final surge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Lecce, the second half will hinge on their ability to transition more effectively from defense to attack, as they need to create clearer scoring opportunities. Milan, meanwhile, must focus on converting their dominance into goals, sharpening their finishing in the final third. The midfield battle will be crucial, with both teams needing to control the tempo to dictate the game’s direction. Substitutions could also play a big role, especially for Lecce if they aim to inject fresh energy into their attack.
